_doc: Paradata registry (London Charter / Seville Principles): the reasoning behind modeled or inferred choices. Facts reference these by id in `paradataRef` instead of repeating prose.

pd-gallery-gradient.rationale: The gallery is modeled near-level at ~0.1% (10 cm per 100 m), the documented constant fall from P9 to P-5, with the two documented stepped falls (1 m under P4, 1.2 m under P6). Step magnitudes are ~1 m and therefore sub-visible at scene scale, so they are shown as annotated markers rather than literal geometry.
pd-gallery-gradient.author: project team

pd-schematic-positions.rationale: Per-shaft plan positions (modelPos) are read as relative along-route spacing from fig.3-1 (the 3D block model, which carries a 100 m scale bar) and cross-checked against fig.5-3 (schematic longitudinal section) and the documented inter-shaft distances (see gallery.documentedDistances). The along-route scale is calibrated at ~7 m per model x-unit via the documented P2–P3 = 28 m (modelPos x 4→8). Under that scale the auxiliary shafts P-5A/P-7A were re-spaced (2026-07-02) so P-7A sits ~25 m NW of P-5 as documented (previously ~45 m — the over-spacing flagged in review). fig.3-1 is an oblique 3D perspective, so spacing elsewhere is approximate, NOT georeferenced. Positions are declared in a `model-schematic` CRS; real WGS84 coordinates (geo) are left null (see pd-source-precedence). Correction: these positions were previously mis-cited to fig.5-4, which is actually a photograph of shaft P2.
pd-schematic-positions.basis: Waringo 2018, fig.3-1 (p.7, 100 m scale bar); fig.5-3 (p.17); documented distances p.17, p.22
pd-schematic-positions.author: project team

pd-source-precedence.rationale: Source-of-truth hierarchy for this model: (1) the official SIT brochure (Waringo/Faber/Werner 2018) is authoritative for ALL qanat facts — shaft depths, surveyed floor elevations, gallery gradient and steps, geology, hydrology, and the route/spacing arrangement; (2) EU-DEM 25 m (OpenTopoData) is external CONTEXT terrain only — it supplies the surrounding landscape surface, not the qanat, and where it disagrees with the brochure the brochure wins; (3) OpenStreetMap shaft nodes are the lowest-authority, provisional cross-check. Critically, the brochure contains NO coordinate grid on any figure (fig.3-1 has only a 100 m scale bar on an oblique 3D view; fig.5-3 is explicitly exaggerated), so true WGS84 georeferencing CANNOT be derived from it. Therefore per-shaft `geo` stays null and OSM remains the only (provisional) georeference candidate — see pd-osm-georef. This is why the EU-DEM surface at OSM points does not reconcile with the brochure's documented depths: they are different, unmerged sources at different authority levels, not an error to 'fix' in the qanat data.
pd-source-precedence.author: project team

pd-postulated-shafts.rationale: Shafts P-2, P-3 and P-6A are inferred from the regular inter-shaft spacing of the located shafts (in qanats the spacing often matches the depth). They are not excavated or surveyed; existence is `inferred`, position `hypothetical` and schematic.
pd-postulated-shafts.author: project team

pd-nominal-datum.rationale: Vertical authority is the brochure: the surveyed shaft-floor elevations (P-7A 354.3, P-5A 357.5, P-4 355.4 m a.s.l.) and the documented depths are the true datum for the qanat. For non-GeoData terrain modes there is no real surface datum, so a nominal site datum is used (valley ~340 m, plateau ~385 m a.s.l.) purely to place the gallery/shafts at plausible depths. GeoData mode draws the SURFACE from EU-DEM metres (context), but the qanat depths/floors remain those documented by the brochure (see pd-source-precedence).
pd-nominal-datum.basis: Waringo 2018 (floors/depths); nominal surface consistent with Waringo 2018 elevations 350-405 m a.s.l.
pd-nominal-datum.author: project team

pd-osm-georef.rationale: Real shaft coordinates were imported from OpenStreetMap (data/osm-shafts.json, source osm-qanat-shafts) as candidate georeferencing. UPDATE 2026-07-02: cross-checking against the 0.5 m ACT LiDAR 2019 DTM (act-lidar-2019) VALIDATES these coordinates — LiDAR sampled at the OSM points reproduces the brochure's floor+depth surfaces to 0.5–2.5 m (P-4 366.9 vs 367.4; P1 386.1 vs 385; P3 392.5 vs 390; P5 392.1 vs 391) and resolves the Haedchen drop (P6–P9 382→377 m). The earlier apparent mismatch was an EU-DEM 25 m artifact (+10–21 m, plateau-edge averaging), NOT an OSM or brochure error. The OSM '-6..9' → P-label mapping is still inferred and the P2–P3 spacing reads ~20 m vs a documented 28 m, so per-node labels stay medium-confidence, but the coordinates are now trusted enough to drive georeferenced placement + a LiDAR-sampled surface.
pd-osm-georef.basis: OpenStreetMap contributors (ODbL); validated against ACT LiDAR 2019 (act-lidar-2019)
pd-osm-georef.author: project team

pd-strata-render.rationale: The geological cross-section is a mix of sourced facts and display approximation. Sourced: the stratigraphic sequence and names (Luxembourg sandstone / marl+limestone / Keuper mudstone / dolomitic marl, fig.4-3), the ~2% SE dip (p.14), and that groundwater accumulates at the sandstone/marl interface and the gallery floor sits at that boundary (p.6, p.14, fig.5-24). Rendered faithfully: in GeoData mode the sandstone/marl interface (= groundwater table) is drawn ~2 m below the near-level gallery floor at REAL elevation, and the weathered cover uses the cited ~2 m. Approximate (NOT yet from the cited ranges): the marl and Keuper sub-thicknesses (~16 m, ~6 m display values) and the exact interface dip — these are decorative depths pending a digitised fig.4-2/4-3 section. Non-GeoData (procedural) mode uses purely schematic scene-unit bands.
pd-strata-render.author: project team

pd-aux-system.rationale: P-5A and P-7A belong to a separate, higher, dry auxiliary channel ~30 m north of the main line; their channel floor sits above the aquiferous qanat channel, so they were never connected to it and are drawn as a distinct dashed channel.
pd-aux-system.author: project team
